title: Delegate cockpit open to the Herdr seam
goal: Remove the duplicate Herdr protocol implementation from the operator-side cockpit open/focus command so Khazad-Doom has exactly one Herdr protocol layer behind the Cockpit seam.
acceptance: The operator-side cockpit open/focus command delegates to the same Cockpit/CockpitAdapter seam used by daemon run and worker-pane launch paths.

src/cli.rs no longer implements Herdr protocol details directly: no Herdr workspace list/create/focus JSON protocol parsing, no root_pane.pane_id extraction, and no standalone Herdr version probe outside the cockpit seam.
The explicit cockpit open/focus command preserves its JSON output shape, fallback/remediation behavior, latest-run lookup behavior, and operator command suggestions.
There is one Herdr protocol implementation in the crate, localized to src/workflow/cockpit.rs or a strictly internal child of that seam.
A regression test or grep guard would catch reintroducing direct Herdr protocol helpers in src/cli.rs.
No workflow truth path changes: Herdr remains optional observability/focus only, and daemon-owned state remains authoritative for run status, verification, merge, and handoff.
must_ask_if: The implementation would add a second Herdr adapter or compatibility layer instead of deleting/delegating the duplicate CLI protocol code.
The implementation would make Herdr required for status, handoff, blocker answers, verification, or headless operation.
The implementation would parse Herdr pane text, scrollback, or agent-status metadata as correctness evidence.
The implementation would change the public JSON contract of khazad-doom cockpit open in a way not implied by delegating to the Cockpit seam.
